item.title: Aspiring teachers demand immediate govt pry school appointment
item.description: The aspiring teachers, finally recommended for the position of assistant teachers in government primary schools, demanded their immediate appointment on Sunday....

item.title: Signal attacked by hackers allegedly linked to Russia
item.description: Signal, an end-to-end encrypted messaging app long considered one of the most secure in the world, has recently faced attacks from hackers accused of links to Russia......

item.title: Rangamati DC cautions rights activist Rani Yen Yen
item.description: Rangamati district administration has accused human rights activist and Chakma Circle Rani Yen Yen of spreading ‘false’ and ‘misleading’ information against the government and the military....

item.title: PM directs fiscal austerity, strict scrutiny of projects
item.description: State minister for planning Zonayed Abdur Rahim Saki on Sunday said that prime minister Tarique Rahman had issued a set of rigorous directives aimed at ensuring fiscal discipline and accountability in the government’s development projects....

item.title: Tigers set long-term goals in T20s
item.description: The Bangladesh national team are set to return to the T20I format after a five-month gap with some fresh faces onboard when they take on New Zealand in the first of the three-match series at the Bir Sreshtho Shahid Flight Lieutenant Matiur Rahman Stadium in Chattogram today......

item.title: Litton embraces different roles in different formats
item.description: Litton Das is comfortable to embrace different roles in different formats as Bangladesh begins the T20I series against New Zealand, starting at the Bir Sreshtho Shahid Flight Lieutenant Matiur Rahman Stadium in Chattogram today......

item.title: Tanzid, Amite earn maiden Test call-ups
item.description: Tanzid Hasan and Amite Hasan have earned their maiden call-ups as the Bangladesh Cricket Board announced the Test squad for the first game against Pakistan on Sunday......Environmental news updates

item.title: City reach FA Cup final
item.description: Nico Gonzalez’s stunning strike fired Manchester City into a record fourth consecutive FA Cup final as Pep Guardiola’s men produced a late fightback to beat Southampton 2-1 on Saturday......

item.title: Govt appoints new heads at 15 offices
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government continued its reshuffle in the civil administration as it on Sunday appointed new heads, including directors general and chairmen, to 15 key institutions under different ministries....

item.title: Cumilla crowned NCC Champions
item.description: Cumilla clinched the title of the 44th National Cricket Championship as they beat Dhaka by 39 runs in a rain-hit final at the BKSP Ground-3 on Sunday......

item.title: RNPP reactor to be fuelled tomorrow
item.description: The country is going to load uranium into one of the two reactors of its first nuclear power plant at Iswardi in Pabna tomorrow against the backdrop of severe power outages causing people to suffer and businesses to face production losses....City & Local Guides

item.title: Heated exchanges in JS over political intolerance
item.description: A tense parliamentary debate on Sunday featured exchanges between government and opposition lawmakers over allegations ofpoliticalrepression, rising intolerance and a return to divisive political practices despite the current Jatiya Sangsad having been formed......

item.title: 12 YEARS OF NARAYANGANJ SEVEN MURDERS: No end to cry for justice yet
item.description: Twelve years after the abduction and killing of seven people by some members of the Rapid Action Battalion in Narayanganj and Narsingdi, the victims’ families are still waiting for justice, with appeals pending before the apex court for seven years....

item.title: Latham backs domestic and franchise experience
item.description: New Zealand skipper Tom Latham backed the domestic experience of the inexperienced side to face Bangladesh in the T20I series, starting at the Bir Sreshtho Shahid Flight Lieutenant Matiur Rahman Stadium in Chattogram......

item.title: PM to visit Jashore today
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman will visit Jashore today to inaugurate different development projects, lay foundation stone of a medical college and join a street rally....

item.title: Bangladesh women qualify for Asian Games
item.description: Bangladesh national women’s hockey team made history by securing a spot in the Asian Games for the first time after a 2-1 victory over Hong Kong in their Pool A match of the Qualifiers tournament at the GBK Hockey Field in Jakarta, Indonesia on Sunday......

item.title: Arteta in red card rant after Arsenal regain top spot
item.description: Arsenal are back on top of the Premier League, but Mikel Arteta claimed their title challenge could be ruined by two rejected red card decisions in their games against Manchester City and Newcastle......

item.title: Atletico build confidence but Barrios hurt
item.description: Alexander Sorloth struck twice as Atletico Madrid built confidence for their Champions League semi-final clash against Arsenal with a 3-2 win over Athletic Bilbao on Saturday in La Liga......

item.title: Confusion, delusion in Iran war
item.description: THE closure of the Strait of Hormuz following the temporary ceasefire is the culmination of an American policy defined by strategic incoherence. At the centre stands Donald Trump, whose shifting positions, confused war objectives, and conflicting actions have not only failed to ease regional tensions but have actively deepened them......

item.title: Democratic renewal and doctrinal frontiers
item.description: THE Appellate Division’s 2025 review judgement revisiting the 13th amendment originally adjudicated in Abdul Mannan Khan v government of Bangladesh (64 DLR (AD) (2012) 169) marks a pivotal moment in the constitutional evolution of Bangladesh. The decision is not just a routine exercise of review jurisdiction; it is an ambitious attempt by the court to bring......Environmental news updates

item.title: Decentralising power for energy security
item.description: BANGLADESH’S energy security is under increasing strain amid a volatile global and regional context. The country’s heavy dependence on imported energy — particularly through routes such as the Strait of Hormuz — has left it exposed to external shocks. The Russia–Ukraine war, ongoing geopolitical tensions, and instability in global energy markets have intensified......

item.title: Banking carbon in delta
item.description: IN THIS low-lying delta, where rivers braid through fertile plains and cyclones routinely test resilience, an invisible but decisive struggle is underway. It is not fought with embankments or shelters, but with trees, soils and coastal ecosystems that quietly capture and store carbon. As the global climate crisis intensifies, the concepts of carbon stock and carbon sequestration......

item.title: Making classrooms playful
item.description: THE minister of state for primary and mass education at a recent consultation of the Campaign for Popular Education has outlined a direction for primary education. Classrooms should become more playful, child-centred and rooted in storytelling. The idea reflects a wider understanding in education that children learn better when they are engaged and emotionally......

item.title: Cinema culture dies with decline in single screens
item.description: SINGLE-SCREEN cinemas, once a major place of entertainment for low- and middle-income people, have disappeared over the years. In the mid-1990s, there were more than 1,450 cinemas, but the industry began collapsing around 2000, with single-screen halls declining by more than 95 per cent. By 2010, the number had dropped to 742 and it now stands at around......

item.title: Extremism needs to be also fought politically, culturally
item.description: FEARS of attacks by banned extremist organisations on important establishments such as the national assembly building and army and police establishments, places of worship and recreation centres, as a police headquarters release warns, have raised concern. On the receipt of information from an intelligence agency, the police have rightly taken measures to......

item.title: Policy stability, tax reforms key to attract FDI: experts
item.description: Stable and predictable fiscal policies are required as frequent tax changes, tax system complexities and weak governance undermines Bangladesh’s investment climate, economists and foreign investors said on Sunday......Sports news updates

item.title: Businesses for removal of 10pc tax on export stimuli
item.description: Leaders of the apparel and textile sectors on Sunday urged the National Board of Revenue to withdraw the 10 per cent tax imposed on export incentives, arguing that the levy undermines the very purpose of the cash support......

item.title: Chinese investors urged to invest in man-made fibre
item.description: Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association president Mahmud Hasan Khan Babu on Sunday urged Chinese investors to invest at man-made fibre, technical textile and synthetic fibre sector in Bangladesh......

item.title: BB appoints administrator to Aviva Finance
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has appointed an administrator to the troubled non-bank financial institution Aviva Finance, formerly controlled by the controversial S Alam Group, in a move aimed at restoring its operations and governance......

item.title: Bangladesh talks trade, investment with Switzerland
item.description: Bangladesh and Switzerland discussed ways to strengthen bilateral trade, investment, and broader economic cooperation during a meeting held at the secretariat on Sunday......Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: Meherpur farmers face crisis over diesel shortages, long queues
item.description: FARMERS at Gangni upazila in Meherpur district are facing growing difficulties in securing diesel as fuel shortages and long queues continue at several filling stations, raising concerns over irrigation and agricultural production during the peak cultivation period......

item.description: Bangladeshi companies have concluded a successful participation in Techtextil 2026 and Texprocess 2026, reinforcing the country’s growing footprint in the global textile and apparel value chain......

item.title: Customers hit by power outages to get free 1GB data from Banglalink
item.description: Customers in different parts of the country facing frequent network disruptions due to ongoing electricity outages will receive 1GB free data, mobile phone operator Banglalink announced on Sunday......

item.description: Community Bank Bangladesh PLC and Limerick Distribution Limited signed an agreement recently marking a significant milestone in strengthening financial support for micro, small, and medium enterprises across Bangladesh......

item.title: Fuel rationing not applicable to police, emergency services: PM
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Sunday told the parliament that the fuel rationing system was no longer applicable to police and emergency services following a government decision to lift the restriction two days back....Breaking news alerts

item.description: The ferry movement on the Paturia–Daulatdia route in the River Padma was disrupted for about an hour due to rough weather on Sunday....

item.description: The Executive Committee of the National Economic Council on Sunday approved 14 development projects with a total estimated cost of Tk 13,445.41 crore....

item.title: Businesses for investor-friendly budget, lower real estate registration costs
item.description: Business leaders and industry stakeholders on Sunday called for a pragmatic, investment-friendly national budget for fiscal year 2026–27, stressing the need for entrepreneur-oriented tax policies, reduced registration costs in housing sector and stronger energy security......

item.description: A total of 34,054 examinees were absent on Sunday, the third day of the ongoing Secondary School Certificate and its equivalent examinations....

item.description: Stocks began the week on a positive note on Sunday, driven by selective accumulation in December-closing stocks amid expectations of strong earnings performance......

item.description: Sri Lanka’s government issued a call on Sunday for investments in a currently loss-making airport, after a 30-year lease agreement with an Indo-Russian joint venture had failed to take off......

item.description: Authorities suspended a female demonstrator of a government college in Rajshahi on Sunday over allegations of misconduct following violent altercations on the campus....
